title: cornus asperifolia
intro: Cornus asperifolia, also known as the roughleaf dogwood, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to the southeastern United States. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its berries, bark, and roots, which are rich in flavonoids, tannins, and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Cornus asperifolia include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. The herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and extracts, which can be used to make tinctures or salves for topical application.
